comments.text: Descoped for Octopus (octo-h7b shipped bdd7536f): `ticket_show` with `recursive: true` now walks the subtree in Elixir and returns `{root, tickets, tree, deps, ready}` — `tree` and `deps` are graphviz DOT strings embedded in the JSON response, so agents read them directly. Still useful for `bw` CLI users; demoting to P3.

description:
Beadwork has subtree traversal primitives for `ready`/`blocked` (`buildSubtreeOverlay`, `buildSubtreeSet`, `LoadEdges` in `internal/issue/blocking.go`). These are not exposed via a generic show verb.

**Fix:** Add either `bw tree <id>` or `bw show <id> --recursive` that walks the subtree and renders in one of three formats:

- **`--format markdown` (default)** — human-readable output for terminals. The parent/child hierarchy renders as a pretty-printed ascii tree (box-drawing chars: `├──`, `└──`, `│`) inside a fenced code block, with status/priority badges on each node. A DAG section below calls out blocked_by/blocks edges (including external blockers). Intended for `bw` CLI users eyeballing an epic; also renders cleanly in any markdown viewer.
- **`--format json`** — machine-readable `{root, tickets, tree, deps, ready}` payload with `tree`/`deps` as embedded graphviz DOT strings (same shape Octopus `ticket_show --recursive` returns, so CLI and MCP outputs align).
- **`--format dot`** — raw graphviz output, single graph combining parent/child + blocks edges (distinct edge styles) so users can pipe straight into `dot -Tpng`.

**Acceptance:**
- `bw show <epic> --recursive` (no `--format`) prints the markdown tree: a fenced code block containing the ascii tree, followed by a DAG section.
- `bw show <epic> --recursive --format json` matches Octopus's `ticket_show(recursive: true)` payload shape byte-for-byte where possible (at minimum same top-level keys).
- `bw show <epic> --recursive --format dot | dot -Tpng -o epic.png` produces a renderable graph.
- Epic with 3 levels of descendants renders correctly in all three modes.
- DAG view shows external blocker arrows (a child blocked by a ticket outside the subtree).
title: bw show --recursive / bw tree <id> — subtree/DAG rendering
